title: The One with the Stripper

jokes.text: Sure, yeah, he's kind of sexy. Oh, no, I'll be there too. Okay, so we'll just come up with a signal. If it's going well, you can take off.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks Phoebe to come to dinner with her dad for support
jokes.punchline: Phoebe misinterprets this as a romantic opportunity with Rachel's father
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe misunderstanding that feels organic to her character's quirky worldview

jokes.text: He said if he ever saw me doing that again, he would make me eat the entire pack.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why her father is scary, using smoking as example
jokes.punchline: The extreme and bizarre punishment threatened by her father
jokes.explanation: Establishes Dr. Green's intimidating character while being absurd enough to be funny

jokes.text: Gosh, I'm not gonna let that man make you eat your baby.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's promise to support Rachel against her intimidating father
jokes.punchline: Extending the 'eating cigarettes' threat to the baby situation in Phoebe's literal mind
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - takes the literal threat and applies it in a completely different context

jokes.text: Her what?
jokes.setup: Monica's shocked reaction to stripper revelation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's confused reaction to learning about a 'secret' party
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that sets up the bigger reveal, functional but not special

jokes.text: She untied his G-string with her teeth. Somebody stop me.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to downplay the bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Phoebe revealing embarrassing details then mock-dramatically asking to be stopped
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Phoebe's dramatic flair, makes Monica's situation worse

jokes.text: Well, you could have untied it with your hands.
jokes.setup: Monica defending that she had no choice in the party activities
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical observation about alternative methods
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deadpan logic pointing out the obvious choice Monica ignored

jokes.text: You went home with the waitress. Ha. Yeah, that was a good night.
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about the 'boring' steak dinner bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he actually scored, followed by Joey's fond remembrance
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal showing Chandler's hypocrisy, with Joey's typical horndog response

jokes.text: Ugh. I knew giving you that book was gonna come back and bite me in the ass.
jokes.setup: Chandler quoting marriage advice about honesty and openness
jokes.punchline: Monica realizing her gift to him is being used against her
jokes.explanation: Good irony about good intentions backfiring, establishes ongoing marriage book dynamic

jokes.text: Oh, I don't eat meat. It's chicken. Yeah, I don't eat that either. I'll never understand you lesbians.
jokes.setup: Dr. Green ordering chicken for Phoebe who says she doesn't eat meat
jokes.punchline: His confusion followed by misidentifying Phoebe as a lesbian
jokes.explanation: Shows Dr. Green's narrow worldview and tendency to make assumptions

jokes.text: Seventy-four? I ordered the '75. That's a magnificent wine. The '74 is sewage. Why would you bring me sewage? Is that a hard question? Are you an idiot? Is that why you're a waiter?
jokes.setup: Waiter brings wrong vintage of wine
jokes.punchline: Dr. Green's increasingly hostile questioning culminating in occupational insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Dr. Green's intimidating personality that Rachel fears

jokes.text: He's right, though. The '74 is absolute piss.
jokes.setup: Dr. Green's harsh treatment of the waiter over wine vintage
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually agreeing with his wine assessment despite his rudeness
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's unexpected wine knowledge contrasts with her hippie persona

jokes.text: Why? So he can get mad at the baby? Hey, that is the baby's problem.
jokes.setup: Rachel's plan to wait years and let the baby tell Dr. Green about the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Rachel's selfish logic about passing the problem to her unborn child
jokes.explanation: Absurd but logical extension of Rachel's avoidance, with her casual dismissal being funny

jokes.text: I got TiVo. What's TiVo? It's slang for 'pregnant.'
jokes.setup: Rachel panicking about how to tell her father she's pregnant
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's completely made-up definition to 'help' the conversation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd 'help' from Phoebe that makes things worse in character-appropriate way

jokes.text: Well, uh, yes and no. Except, not no. So to sum it up, yeah.
jokes.setup: Dr. Green asking directly if Rachel is pregnant
jokes.punchline: Rachel's convoluted attempt to soften the blow that just confuses things
jokes.explanation: Rachel's panic leading to verbal gymnastics that accomplish nothing

jokes.text: Oh, no. Please don't tell me it's her.
jokes.setup: Learning Ross is the father of Rachel's baby
jokes.punchline: Dr. Green's assumption that Phoebe might be the father
jokes.explanation: Continues Dr. Green's confused assumptions about Phoebe's sexuality

jokes.text: You're gonna be a poppy. That's true. I'm a poppy. I'm gonna be a poppy.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to spin the pregnancy as positive news
jokes.punchline: Dr. Green's delight with the cute grandparent nickname overshadowing everything else
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing how a simple word can completely change someone's perspective

jokes.text: Young lady, don't you sit there and tell me my first grandchild is gonna be a bastard.
jokes.setup: The happy moment about becoming a grandfather
jokes.punchline: Immediate shift to old-fashioned moral outrage about marriage
jokes.explanation: Great tonal whiplash from joy to traditional moral outrage, very in character

jokes.text: February 2nd.
jokes.setup: Dr. Green demanding to know when the wedding will be
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately providing a specific date under pressure
jokes.explanation: Rachel's panic response is believable and sets up future complications

jokes.text: Oh, no, I had my eyes closed so I could concentrate and take it all in.
jokes.setup: Ross noting that Charlie seemed to be falling asleep during his lecture
jokes.punchline: Charlie's overly polite explanation for what was obviously sleeping
jokes.explanation: Standard polite excuse humor, establishes Charlie as trying too hard to be nice

jokes.text: Yeah, a lot of my students do that.
jokes.setup: Charlie's excuse about closing her eyes to concentrate
jokes.punchline: Ross's dry acknowledgment that his lectures put people to sleep
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor from Ross acknowledging his boring reputation

jokes.text: My God, you must be good in bed.
jokes.setup: Ross and Charlie's polite but boring interaction about stones
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt conclusion about why she'd stay interested
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey observation - cutting through politeness to sexual logic

jokes.text: I'm gonna take that book and I'm gonna beat you to death with it.
jokes.setup: Chandler giving relationship advice from marriage book
jokes.punchline: Ross threatening violence with the very book being quoted
jokes.explanation: Good ironic threat using the advice-giving tool as weapon against advice-giver

jokes.text: Am I going in the wife hall of fame or what?
jokes.setup: Monica surprising Chandler with a stripper for his bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Monica's proud expectation of praise for unconventional gift
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive need to be the best wife leads to inappropriate gesture

jokes.text: Sure. One year is paper, but two months is lap dance.
jokes.setup: Monica reframing the stripper as a two-month anniversary gift
jokes.punchline: Joey's mock-traditional gift guide including sexual services
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of anniversary gift traditions with perfect economy and Joey's voice

jokes.text: I understand. Who would cancel an actual date just to go to a fake bachelor party?
jokes.setup: Ross saying he can't attend because he has a date with Mona
jokes.punchline: Monica's passive-aggressive guilt trip about priorities
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitiveness turning into guilt manipulation, in character

jokes.text: I'm sorry I won't be able to make it to your imaginary wedding...but I'm really busy that day. I already have a unicorn baptism and a leprechaun bar mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Rachel finally admitting there's no wedding planned
jokes.punchline: Dr. Green's sarcastic fantasy commitments escalating to mythical creatures
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating sarcasm with creative mythical creature imagery, very quotable

jokes.text: I didn't want him to start yelling at me like I was some '74 Latour. It's Lafite. The '74 Latour is actually drinking quite nicely.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she lied about the wedding
jokes.punchline: Ross correcting her wine knowledge after her wine comparison
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Ross's nerdy knowledge interrupting emotional moment

jokes.text: Yes. He says I'm damaged goods.
jokes.setup: Dr. Green asking if Ross won't marry Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's fake claim about Ross's reason, making him look terrible
jokes.explanation: Rachel's panic lie that will obviously cause problems, good setup for confrontation

jokes.text: ...until you finally have the sweet release of death.
jokes.setup: Joey asking Chandler about never being with another woman
jokes.punchline: Darkly comic view of marriage as eternal imprisonment ending only in death
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark take on marriage from usually shallow Joey, good contrast

jokes.text: So she's a... Yep, that's one naked hooker.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Joey realizing the 'stripper' went to the bedroom
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact confirmation of what they're dealing with
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the mistaken stripper situation with Joey's casual delivery

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Oh, my God, I am so sorry. Ah, forget it. It's from Pier 1.
jokes.setup: Mona breaking what appears to be valuable artifact
jokes.punchline: Ross revealing it's actually cheap furniture store item
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of Ross's pretentious museum persona with mass market reality

jokes.text: You know, this is actually not a great time for me.
jokes.setup: Dr. Green arriving angrily to confront Ross about the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Ross's polite understatement while facing obvious confrontation
jokes.explanation: Ross's awkward politeness in face of obvious danger, good character moment

jokes.text: Yes, yes, I did, but it was just a one-night thing. It meant nothing. Oh? Really? That's what my daughter means to you, nothing?
jokes.setup: Ross trying to downplay the significance of sleeping with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Dr. Green twisting Ross's words to sound even worse
jokes.explanation: Classic trap where honesty makes things worse, good escalation

jokes.text: Could be. I mean, technically, she did strip. We just missed it.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting the hooker might also be a stripper who got confused
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logical observation about technicality of stripping
jokes.explanation: Chandler's logical approach to absurd situation, finding technical truth

jokes.text: That is funny. Maybe for my birthday she'll murder someone.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting Monica might be playing a joke with the hooker
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dark escalation of what Monica might do next
jokes.explanation: Great dark escalation showing Chandler's anxiety about Monica's 'helpful' surprises

jokes.text: Yeah, go ahead. We're gonna have to burn that room down anyway.
jokes.setup: Hooker asking if she can smoke in the room
jokes.punchline: Chandler's resignation that the room is already contaminated beyond saving
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the contamination theme, Chandler's practical despair

jokes.text: Hey, Ross, it's Joey. There's a hooker over here. We thought maybe you'd know something about it. No. No. No. No. No.
jokes.setup: Answering machine message playing while Ross is with Dr. Green and Mona
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual mention of hooker in worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for maximum embarrassment, great use of technology for comedy

jokes.text: Is that what they call strippers sometimes? When they're hookers.
jokes.setup: Monica's confusion about stripper vs hooker terminology
jokes.punchline: Stu's matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Simple explanation humor, showing Monica's naivete about the business

jokes.text: Either that, or she's just the best, most expensive date I ever had.
jokes.setup: Stu confirming the woman is definitely a hooker
jokes.punchline: Alternative explanation suggesting he may have accidentally hired one himself
jokes.explanation: Good alternative interpretation that implicates Stu in similar confusion

jokes.text: Is there anything you're really bad at, you know, sexually?
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting Monica might want Chandler to 'learn something' from hooker
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt question about Chandler's sexual deficiencies
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey bluntness about sex, perfectly in character

jokes.text: All right, rock, paper, scissors for who has to tell the whore to leave. What? I miss this.
jokes.setup: Trying to decide who should ask the hooker to leave
jokes.punchline: Joey's nostalgic reaction to their game-playing
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing Joey missing their friendship rituals despite circumstances

jokes.text: Remember? You and me used to be inseparable. Well, now it's like things are different.
jokes.setup: Rock paper scissors moment triggering nostalgia
jokes.punchline: Joey's heartfelt observation about their changed friendship
jokes.explanation: More character moment than joke, but Joey's emotional honesty in hooker situation is amusing

jokes.text: Listen, I am this close to robbing you guys.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Joey having emotional heart-to-heart
jokes.punchline: Hooker's practical concern about wasted time threatening theft
jokes.explanation: Good interruption of sentimental moment with practical criminal logic

jokes.text: She's a hooker! She's a hooker! She's a...!
jokes.setup: Monica arriving as Chandler is about to explain the situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's panicked repetitive shouting being cut off
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with Chandler's panic, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: 'We were on a break.' 'No we weren't.' What happened to you two?
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel having a mild disagreement instead of fighting
jokes.punchline: Phoebe referencing their famous recurring argument about their relationship break
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-callback to one of the show's most famous running arguments

jokes.text: I get it. But, Mona, what relationship is not complicated? We all have our baggage. You must too. Why else would you still be single?
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to reassure Mona about Ross being a good choice
jokes.punchline: Rachel accidentally insulting Mona while trying to help
jokes.explanation: Classic foot-in-mouth moment where helping makes things worse

jokes.text: I'm so gonna leave right now.
jokes.setup: Rachel's backhanded insult about why Mona is single
jokes.punchline: Mona's calm but definitive response to the insult
jokes.explanation: Good understatement showing Mona's dignity while exiting gracefully

jokes.text: ...but I thought it was better that you heard it from Rachel's father.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why he didn't tell Mona about Rachel and the baby
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic reference to the worst possible way to find out
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about how badly the revelation went

jokes.text: He's a good kisser, isn't he? I'm going.
jokes.setup: Phoebe arriving as Ross and Mona are reconciling with a kiss
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's inappropriate comment driving Mona away again
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe inappropriate timing ruining a moment

jokes.text: Her ass-print is still on your grandmother's quilt. Do you really want to talk about smoking?
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about Monica letting the hooker smoke
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out the greater contamination already present
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and visual imagery, Monica's practical point about priorities

jokes.text: Ooh. These tennis shoes are so tight. I think I'll take them off. Could you not narrate?
jokes.setup: Monica attempting to strip seductively
jokes.punchline: Chandler's request to stop the awkward commentary
jokes.explanation: Monica's awkward attempt at seduction with Chandler's practical feedback

jokes.text: Stupid guy on my phone.
jokes.setup: Phoebe hanging up on Dr. Green mid-argument
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual dismissal of the intimidating man everyone fears
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - completely unbothered by what terrifies everyone else

tmdb_overview: Monica arranges a bachelor party with a stripper after Chandler discovers her bachelorette party had one. Rachel tells her father about her pregnancy.
